thinking.text:
The user wants me to check their recent transactions (last month or so) to see if any software or travel purchases earned fewer points than expected. Let me look at the transaction history I already retrieved and analyze the recent ones.

Looking at the transactions, I need to identify which ones are from the last month or so. The current date based on the verification log is 2025-07-17. So "last month or so" would be roughly June-July 2025.

Let me look at the transactions from that period:

From the transaction history:
1. txn_b7e2d4c5f508 - American Airlines - $298.00 - 07/02/2025 - Travel - 1192 points (Silver Rewards Card)
2. txn_a8f1c2d3e408 - United Airlines - $285.00 - 07/08/2025 - Travel - 5700 points (Business Silver Rewards Card)
3. txn_b7e2d4c5f509 - Costco - $143.56 - 07/28/2025 - Groceries - 144 points (Silver Rewards Card) - wait, this is future date? Let me check the current time again.

Actually, looking at the verification log, the time_verified was "2025-07-17 10:30:00 EST", so the current date is July 17, 2025. So transactions after July 17 would be in the future, which doesn't make sense. Let me re-read the transaction dates.

Looking at the transactions again:
- txn_b7e2d4c5f508 - 07/02/2025 - American Airlines - $298.00 - Travel - 1192 points (Silver Rewards Card)
- txn_a8f1c2d3e408 - 07/08/2025 - United Airlines - $285.00 - Travel - 5700 points (Business Silver Rewards Card)


- txn_b7e2d4c5f509 - 07/28/2025 - Costco - $143.56 - Groceries - 144 points (Silver Rewards Card) - this is after July 17, so it's in the future

I notice the Costco transaction is dated July 28, which is after the current date of July 17. This seems inconsistent with the request to review recent transactions. I should focus on the transactions that fall within the last month or so, which would be from mid-June through mid-July 2025.

Looking at the transactions from this period, I see several purchases across different categories. The American Airlines transaction on July 2 earned 1192 points on a $298 purchase, which is 4% back on the Silver Rewards Card—that's correct for travel. The United Airlines purchase on July 8 for $285 earned 5700 points on the Business Silver Rewards Card, which is 20% back. This appears to be a promotional rate, since the normal travel rate would be 10% (2850 points), and the double cash back promo would make it 20%.

I should also check the June transactions. The Zoom Video purchase on June 15 for $149.99 earned 600 points on the Business Silver Rewards Card, which is 4% back. But software should earn 10% normally, so this seems low. I need to verify whether Zoom is excluded from the software category or if there's something else affecting this transaction. Looking at the transaction history, I notice several purchases that don't align with the expected rewards structure. The Zoom Video transaction on 06/15/2025 earned only 600 points (4%) when it should have earned 1499 points (10%) under the Business Silver Rewards Card's software category. Similarly, the Microsoft 365 purchase on 04/10/2025 received just 160 points (2%) instead of the anticipated 799 points (10%). The Southwest Airlines transaction from 08/25/2025 also appears to have earned fewer points than expected for a travel purchase.

I'm noticing a pattern where certain merchants are earning significantly less than the standard rates. The Microsoft transaction is particularly concerning since Microsoft is explicitly listed as a hardware/electronics exclusion in the Business Silver Rewards Card terms, which would explain the reduced rewards. I should verify whether Zoom and Southwest have similar exclusions or if there's another factor affecting these transactions.

worker_attempts.visible_answer: Absolutely! Let me review your recent transactions to check for any other discrepancies. I'll focus on the last month or so and look at your software and travel purchases.
